The ideal candidate will have offshore wind power experience however offshore oil and gas experience may be considered. The potential candidate will be able to utilise engineering support services through EirGrid frameworks however the candidate will have a broad understanding of the below specialisations. You’ll be responsible for:* You will ensure robust quality systems are in place and suitably managed in line with service excellence. * You will manage delivery from project inception/brief, through design development and planning, contractor procurement and construction phase in accordance with the overall delivery program and agreed project milestones. * You will manage tasks and associated deliverables in support of the Associate Director and other senior leaders, alongside the wider project and client teams, to ensure the timely and accurate delivery of the project. * Demonstrating the ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, highly collaborative work environment. * Demonstrating enthusiasm to drive development and delivery of large infrastructure projects. * Identifying and managing stakeholders and ensuring regular and timely communications at all levels. * Ability to work collaboratively with customers and stakeholders. * Ensuring that appropriate project controls are in place within the team having regard to budgets, schedule, scope, quality risk and other performance indicators. * Preparing and updating formal project progress reports such as monthly reports including financial, budget and project schedule reporting. * Ability to analyse issues, exercising judgement to make decisions and escalate as appropriate. * Supporting improvement of processes, procedures and systems. * Development of governance papers and reports to support the approval process. * Managing Risks and Issues and escalate appropriately. You’ll need to have:* Extensive geotechnical expertise, including offshore substation platform mechanical and structural experience. * Digitally fluent leader, building trusted client relationships and high‑performing teams. * Chartered or professionally aligned, with strong commercial, project and stakeholder capability. * Sustainability‑minded professional, experienced within complex, large‑scale infrastructure projects. Our values Safety first \- Going home safe and well: We champion a safe, diverse and inclusive working environment, understanding the importance of wellbeing in every team. Client focus \- Deliver on our promise: We own the quality of deliverables, strategic outcomes and build long term relationships with our clients. Integrity \- Always do the right thing: We influence positive outcomes within our industry, while always aligning with our compliance obligations. Create opportunity \- For our people to excel: We champion a continuous improvement culture throughout all activities, inspiring our people and teams to develop. Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfil all the criteria, please apply as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation. We are also open to discussing part time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable with\-in the role. \#LI\-Onsite

title: Client Relationship On-site Representative
description: **Office based role Mon to Fri \- Sandyford, Dublin.** Are you passionate about cutting\-edge financial / payments technology? Do you thrive in a fast\-paced pioneering environment where you bring you know how of introducing technology solutions to the retail and hospitality verticals within Ireland? **About Viva.com** Viva.com is the 1st Tech Bank in Europe for Businesses revolutionizing omnichannel payment acceptance, offering card issuing, loans, and deposit accounts to businesses across Europe. Licensed by the Bank of Greece, it operates under the EU FOS regime in 22 EU countries, delivering seamless financial services. Viva.com is the first to pioneer and holds the top position in the Tap on Any Device technology, enabling payments acceptance on any device or even without a device. Today Viva.com is leading the transition to All\-in\-One business solutions, powered by its full suite of financial services. To that end, reflecting its holistic approach to business needs, Viva.com has built a dynamic ecosystem featuring over 450 tech partner\-innovators in the software and hardware space. Connecting directly to all local payment schemes, alternative payment methods and payment systems across the continent, further strengthens Viva.com's value proposition. Viva.com envisions to provide businesses a value\-driven platform to accelerate the adoption of the latest technology. **Role Overview:** On\-site Representatives are responsible for promoting company's services remotely while providing merchants with the necessary support to ensure business continuity and optimal service usage. **Key Responsibilities:** * Promote company's services through remote interactions; * Assist merchants with verification and activation processes; * Monitor and analyze portfolio performance; * Provide remote support and consultation for existing customers; * Perform scheduled and self\-initiated activities; * Ensure new merchants in the assigned portfolio complete registration; * Enter and maintain up to date client information into the company's database and keep records with relevant data; * Analyze market trends and customer feedback to refine strategies; * Support existing merchants in maintaining active usage; * Escalate issues by opening cases for problems beyond their resolution scope; * Present product features and benefits to clients in a compelling manner. **Requirements** * 2\+ years of experience in business development within a fast\-paced environment preferably a financial services institution or similar SaaS solution; * Good knowledge of relevant computer programs (e.g. CRM software), social networking (LinkedIn) and telephone systems; * Excellent communication skills (verbal and written); * Excellent level of English; * Ability to work independently and under pressure; * A positive approach to objection handling; * Good organizational skills and attention to detail **Benefits** * Bonus, capped at an additional 18 months salary per annum * Private medical health insurance * 25 Days holiday \+ bank holidays * A diverse multi\-cultural working environment * Local office based in Dublin * 9am\-5:30pm working hours Monday to Friday

title: Clinical Services Psychologist  /Psychotherapist
description: **Aware Clinical Services Psychologist/Psychotherapist** **Job Overview:** We are seeking a compassionate, innovative, and enthusiastic psychologist or psychotherapist to join our friendly and dedicated team in the NGO sector. This is a new role offering an exciting opportunity to support the strategic clinical leadership across the range of services offered and shape high\-quality mental health service delivery for people living with depression, bipolar, and anxiety. **About Aware:** Aware is the national organisation providing free support, education, information and therapeutic services to people impacted by depression, bipolar and anxiety. We play a vital role in offering free mental health services across the country to the public. Aware works with a large team of mental health professionals and volunteers and training partners to deliver our wide range of vital services. **Our Vision** Aware undertakes to create a society where people affected by stress, depression, bipolar disorder and mood related conditions are understood, supported, free from stigma, and are encouraged to access appropriate therapies. **Our Values** Excellence, Compassion, Accountability and Integrity. **About the role** Reporting to Aware’s Clinical Director (Senior Clinical Psychologist), key aspects of the role include (but are not limited to): * To ensure the clinical effectiveness of Aware counselling and psychoeducation programmes by supporting clinical audit and other service development activities related to the services. * To contribute to creating a psychologically informed framework of service delivery across Aware Services * To provide clinical consultation/guidance and, where required, clinical supervision to Aware clinicians and service managers * To undertake direct assessment and triage with new service users to the Aware Counselling Service, in line with best practice. * To hold a small caseload, offering brief psychological therapy to adults presenting with mild to moderate depression and anxiety * To conduct risk assessments and implement risk management plans as appropriate * To contribute to research in line with the Aware Research Strategy and overall objectives * To plan and deliver teaching and training on workplace mental health workshops in line with best practice and current evidence base. * To represent Aware in public, policy, and sector\-wide mental health discussions. * To work with the communications department utilising the sector wide research / insights to inform our evidence\-informed communications strategy * Support the Clinical Director in offering external\-facing psychological leadership, specifically engaging state bodies and medical stakeholders to represent, promote, and advance Aware strategic objectives. Essential Skills and Experience: * Have an Irish post\-graduate professional psychology qualification (Doctorate or equivalent) accredited by the Psychological Society of Ireland in Clinical or Counselling Psychology. Candidates qualified overseas must provide evidence of validation by the Department of Health at the time of application. Or * Psychotherapist fully accredited by the Irish Council for Psychotherapy (ICP) or the Irish Association for Counselling and Psychotherapy (IACP) * For psychologists, at least 5 years’ experience in professional psychology, with no less than 3 years spent in gaining the post graduate qualification and at least 1 year full time in post qualification experience as a professional psychologist. * For psychotherapists a minimum of five years post qualification/accreditation experience working in health and social care, therapeutic or related organisational settings supporting people with depression and anxiety * Commitment to evidence\-based practice and client\-centred care. * Effective presentation and facilitation skills including the ability to present information in a clear and concise manner to a wide range of audiences * Excellent communication, teamwork, and interpersonal skills. * Comfortable with a wide range of IT systems. Desirable: * Experience of the not\-for\-profit sector * Experience of delivering and evaluating brief therapeutic interventions * Interest in service development and creation of psychoeducational and therapeutic resources in mental health Package * This is a permanent post. * Salary will be commensurate with qualifications and experience. The salary range for this role will be €52,000 – €60,000 pro rata (0\.6 FTE / 22\.5 hours per week) * Aware is an equal opportunities employer. * The role is office based in 9 Upper Leeson Street, Dublin 4, offering a hybrid work model To apply please submit a one\-page cover letter outlining your suitability for the role and a CV (2 pages max) to info@aware.ie *before 5pm on 12th May* . Informal enquiries to Dr Susan Brannick on 01 661 7211\. Interviews will be held the week commencing 18th May.

title: Clinical Specialist Physiotherapist - Pectus Service
description: **Job Description** **Job Title: Clinical Specialist Physiotherapist \- Pectus Service** **Department: Physiotherapy** **Reports to: Allied Therapy Manager** **Date: 2026** 10 hours per week **Overall Purpose of Job** We will provide exceptional patient care in an environment where quality, respect, caring and compassion are at the centre of all we do. **Key Responsibilities** **1\. PROFESSIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES:** **1\.1\.1\.** Co\-ordinate and deliver an effective and efficient Pectus Physiotherapy Service, based on assessment of needs. **1\.1\.2\.** Develop and administer the highest quality of service by identifying and implementing operational procedures in line with best practice standards. **1\.1\.3\.** Keep abreast with new developments, skills and technology to ensure continuing competence in the job. **1\.1\.4\.** Enhance his/her skills in order to initiate and respond to changes in the work environment as additional roles may be demanded e.g. data analysis, clinical audit, continued professional development and research. **2\. MANAGEMENT RESPONSIBILITIES:** **2\.1\.1\.** To participate in the management of the Physiotherapy Service as a member of the Senior Physiotherapy Management team in co\-operation with the Allied Therapy Manager. **2\.1\.2\.** To create and promote good working relationships through open communication. **2\.1\.3\.** To contribute to and participate in the staff induction, staff appraisal and in\-service training programmes. **2\.1\.4\.** To actively participate in and promote continuing education and research activities consistent with the position. **2\.1\.5\.** To maintain appropriate records and statistics sufficient for organisational and legal purposes to include work related injuries/incidents. **2\.1\.6\.** Toregularly review and update Pectus Service Policies and Standard Operating Procedure documents. **2\.1\.7\.** To assist the Allied Therapy Manager in achieving Quality and Accreditation requirements for Physiotherapy. **2\.1\.8\.** To provide in intra disciplinary education to promote physiotherapy service in Beacon Hospital. **2\.1\.9\.** To provide flexible assistance to the Allied Therapy Manager in delivering clinical care and in operational roles such as Policy and Procedure Document updates, Beacon Hospital committee participation where required. **3\. STAFF MANAGEMENT** **3\.1\.1\.** To lead by example a professional, dedicated and punctual team. **3\.1\.2\.** To encourage professional development of team members through promotion of self\-learning, formal courses and in\-service training. **NOTE 1\.** The hours of work are at the discretion of the Physiotherapy Manager and in line with service needs. **Person Specification** **Qualifications** Candidates must hold a degree in Physiotherapy, be registered with CORU and membership of the Irish Society of Chartered Physiotherapists is desirable. **Experience** (Amount and Type) Candidates must hold at least 5 years post graduate experience in the area of Orthopaedic or Musculoskeletal physiotherapy. A suitable postgraduate qualification in Orthopaedics, Musculoskeletal practice is desirable. **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge** In addition to the responsibilities and core competencies of a senior physiotherapist: ***The candidate must have/display the following attributes:*** Applies expert clinical knowledge and reasoning skills in assessment, planning, Implementation and evaluation of patient interventions Carries out clinical skills in the area in keeping with current evidence\-based practice Delivers a holistic patient focused approach and embraces the MDT perspective Applies evidence\-based outcome measures to evaluate efficacy of treatment and Quality of care Balances clinical demands with other responsibilities. * Demonstrates an educational role for example as a 'mentor. * Participates in research. * Generates new knowledge and skills in the specified area of Physiotherapy. * Disseminates advanced ethical awareness and responsibilities in specific areas. * Demonstrates leadership and an understanding of service planning. Demonstrates a critical understanding of the context in which practice occurs for example social, political and inter professional collaboration. Strong evidence of Continuing Professional Development \- Strength and Conditioning etc. is desirable **Personal Competencies** All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager of hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**

title: Cloud DevOps Engineer
description: What you are expected to have: * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field with 10\-15 years of overall IT experience. * Minimum of 5\-10 years of relevant experience in cloud engineering and DevOps. * In\-depth knowledge and hands\-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud. * Proficient in infrastructure\-as\-code (IaC) tools, such as Terraform or CloudFormation or ARM. * Solid understanding of containerization technologies, particularly Docker and Kubernetes especially EKS, ECS and AKS. * Experience in implementing and managing CI/CD pipelines using tools like Jenkins, GitLab CI, Bamboo or CircleCI. * Strong scripting skills in languages like Python, Shell, or PowerShell. * Familiarity with configuration management tools such as Ansible or Puppet. * Knowledge of networking principles and security best practices in cloud environments. * Deploying AI solutions on AWS using AWS Bedrock, Sagemaker etc. * Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (Preferred). * Relevant certifications in cloud platforms and DevOps tools (Preferred). * Experience in an IT consulting services environment (Preferred). * Advanced proficiency in cloud\-native services and serverless architectures (Preferred). * Knowledge of monitoring and logging tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, or Splunk (Preferred). * Expertise in optimizing cloud costs and resource management (Preferred). * Strong communication skills with the ability to convey technical concepts to non\-technical stakeholders (Preferred). * Active participation in the open\-source community or contributions to relevant projects (Preferred). You are will do: * Collaborate with clients and internal teams to understand and analyze their technical requirements. * Design, implement, and maintain cloud infrastructure solutions with a focus on scalability, security, and performance. * Implement and optimize CI/CD pipelines using DevOps best practices. * Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to cloud infrastructure and deployments. * Stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies in cloud computing and DevOps. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure successful delivery of cloud\-based projects. * Provide technical support and guidance to junior team members.

title: Cloud Security Engineer (Kubernetes / Multi-Cloud)
description: We’re looking for a hands\-on Cloud Security Analyst with deep expertise in cloud\-native and container security. This role sits at the core of securing large\-scale, multi\-cloud and Kubernetes\-driven environments. You will play a critical role in defining security standards, implementing hardened baselines, and enhancing detection and response capabilities—working closely with DevOps, Networking, and SOC teams to embed security into every layer of design and automation. 🧩 Key Responsibilities Strengthen and optimize cloud\-native security controls across IAM, network security (WAF, VPC), and data protection (KMS, Vault) Deploy and manage CSPM, CNAPP, and CWPP solutions to maintain continuous security posture across multi\-cloud and Kubernetes environments Integrate cloud telemetry (GCP Audit Logs, AWS CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Kubernetes audit logs) into SIEM/SOAR platforms for centralized visibility Define and enforce cloud security architectures and guardrails aligned with Zero Trust and Least Privilege principles Develop and maintain Security\-as\-Code practices using Terraform, CloudFormation, or Bicep, including policy\-as\-code frameworks Own and enhance the security posture of containerized environments (GKE, AKS, EKS), including image, registry, and runtime security Harden Kubernetes clusters through RBAC, NetworkPolicies, Admission Controllers, and secure configurations Lead cloud security incident response activities including triage, containment, and forensic investigations Continuously monitor emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and attack vectors within cloud\-native ecosystems 🎯 Required Qualifications Experience 8\+ years in Information Security Minimum 8\+ years in Cloud Security At least 6\+ years working with Kubernetes / container security Cloud Expertise Hands\-on experience with at least two major cloud platforms (GCP and Azure preferred; AWS/OCI a plus) Container \& Kubernetes Security Strong understanding of Kubernetes security controls (RBAC, Secrets, Ingress, TLS) Familiarity with container runtime security and orchestration Technical Skills Proficiency in scripting (Python preferred; Bash or Go is a plus) Working knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and automation Security Frameworks \& Compliance Knowledge of ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIST, and CIS Benchmarks Certifications (Preferred) CISSP CKS (Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist) GCP Professional Cloud Security Engineer Microsoft Azure Security Engineer / Cybersecurity Architect AWS Certified Security – Specialty Soft Skills Strong stakeholder management and ability to influence engineering teams Experience working in global or multi\-national environments ⭐ Nice to Have Experience securing microservices, APIs, and serverless architectures Background in penetration testing or red teaming (cloud/container focus) Experience in large\-scale SaaS, networking, or cloud\-first organizations Additional certifications across GCP, Azure, AWS, or OCI * Kubernetes certifications such as CKA or advanced security\-focused credentials

title: Cloud Solutions Architect (Daily Rate)
description: **Cloud Solutions Architect** **Daily Rate Contract (1 day onsite)** My Dublin based client is seeking an experienced Cloud Solutions Architect for a daily rate contract. The Cloud Architect will support a large\-scale digital and cloud transformation programme. This is a daily rate contract role focused on designing, delivering, and optimising secure, scalable cloud infrastructure solutions across AWS and/or Azure environments. You will operate within a Cloud Centre of Excellence style function, contributing hands\-on architecture expertise, establishing best practice patterns, and accelerating cloud adoption across enterprise platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Design and deliver secure, scalable, and cost\-efficient cloud architecture solutions aligned to enterprise and regulatory requirements * Refine and translate business requirements into robust technical designs * Provide strategic guidance on cloud adoption, platform optimisation, and architectural best practice * Drive innovation and cost optimisation across public cloud environments * Present architectural solutions to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Ensure security\-by\-design principles are embedded across all solutions * Develop reusable, repeatable cloud patterns to accelerate delivery and reduce platform overhead * Collaborate with engineering teams to implement, deploy, and enhance cloud capabilities * Ensure solutions meet performance, reliability, and governance standards **Required Experience** * 5\+ years’ experience designing and implementing scalable cloud solutions * Strong background in modern cloud\-native architecture (microservices, serverless, containerisation) * Hands\-on Infrastructure as Code expertise (Terraform, CloudFormation) * Deep experience with at least one major cloud platform (AWS or Azure) * Experience designing or implementing Kubernetes solutions (preferably EKS or equivalent) * Proven ability to manage and optimise public cloud infrastructure environments * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills * Experience producing clear, structured architecture and design documentation **Desirable Experience** * Exposure to regulated or complex enterprise environments * Experience across both AWS and Azure * CI/CD and DevOps implementation using IaC tooling * Experience supporting cloud migration or transformation programmes **Certifications (Advantageous)** * AWS Certified Solutions Architect (or equivalent cloud certification)

title: Cloud Support Engineer
description: **Job Description:** Our client, a leading financial services technology organisation, is seeking a Senior Cloud Support Engineer to join their service delivery function, supporting and maintaining a global cloud\-based platform. **Job Responsibilities** * Provide high\-quality technical support to end users, ensuring service levels and customer satisfaction targets are consistently achieved * Deploy, manage and optimise cloud\-based infrastructure to ensure performance, scalability and security across production environments * Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using modern tooling to support efficient software delivery * Monitor system performance, availability and capacity, proactively addressing issues and improving resilience * Manage system upgrades, patching activities and configuration changes across environments * Act as an escalation point for complex technical issues and drive timely resolution * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to deliver reliable and scalable technical solutions * Develop and maintain infrastructure as code using tools such as Terraform * Implement monitoring, logging and observability practices across platforms * Support incident, problem and change management processes aligned to ITIL frameworks * Drive continuous improvement through automation, optimisation and operational best practices **Experience Required** * Minimum 5 years’ experience in cloud infrastructure, technical operations or a similar role * Proven experience supporting highly available, 24/7 cloud\-based environments * Strong hands\-on experience with cloud platforms, particularly Microsoft Azure, alongside exposure to AWS * Experience with cloud\-native architectures and distributed systems * Demonstrated experience in customer\-facing support roles within technical environments * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills across infrastructure and application layers * Experience with monitoring and alerting tools within cloud environments * Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and deployment tooling such as Azure DevOps * Experience with identity and access management principles, including role\-based access controls * Proven ability to manage incidents effectively and maintain composure in high\-pressure situations **Desirable Skills** * Experience with platform security best practices and secure\-by\-design principles * Knowledge of vulnerability management and remediation approaches * Exposure to infrastructure automation and configuration management tools * Experience implementing observability frameworks including logging and metrics collection * Understanding of modern DevOps practices and continuous delivery models * Strong communication skills with the ability to work across global teams **Educational Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline **Working Hours \& Benefits** * Competitive salary and benefits package * Opportunity to work within a global, technology\-driven environment * Exposure to modern cloud\-native technologies and large\-scale platforms * Collaborative and supportive team culture with opportunities for professional growth **How to Apply** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **Similar Roles Open** As a specialised recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities.

title: Construction Plant Fitting Apprenticeship
description: locations Navan time type Full time posted on Posted Today time left to apply End Date: June 20, 2026 (30\+ days left to apply) job requisition id JOB\_015069**Construction Plant Fitting Apprenticeship – Boliden Tara Mines** **Build a skilled, future\-ready career with support, purpose and real opportunities to grow.** At Boliden Tara Mines, we provide the metals essential to improve society for generations to come. We are guided by our values of Care, Courage and Responsibility, and by our ambition to be the most climate\-friendly and respected metal provider in the world. If you enjoy problem solving, technology, and learning how complex systems work \- this apprenticeship could be for you. **Thinking about your next step ?** A **Construction Plant Fitting Apprenticeship** at Tara Mines offers a practical, structured way to **earn while you learn**, gain a nationally recognised qualification, and build skills that are in demand across many industries. On successful completion, apprentices will achieve a **QQI Advanced Certificate – Level 6**, a nationally recognised qualification valued across multiple industries. You don’t need previous experience \- we provide training, mentoring and support from day one. **What you’ll learn and do** **Construction Plant Fitters** support with the maintenance and reliability of mobile plant and equipment used across our mining operations. With guidance from experienced mentors, you’ll learn how these machines operate and why safety, quality and teamwork are critical in everything we do. During your apprenticeship, you’ll be supported to learn how to: * Diagnose faults and carry out repairs on mobile plant and equipment * Perform routine servicing and preventative maintenance to keep equipment operating safely and efficiently * Complete inspections and safety checks in line with mining regulations and site standards * Use modern maintenance systems and technology, including the CMMS system (Maximo) * Develop strong problem‑solving skills through proactive fault‑finding and repair activities **Your support and development** Boliden Tara Mines offers world‑class technical training, along with competitive pay and excellent rewards as you progress and develop your skills. You will be part of a safety\-focused, inclusive team, supported by experienced and qualified craftspeople who will: * Coach and guide you throughout your apprenticeship * Help you build confidence in your technical skills * Encourage questions and continuous learning We take pride in creating an environment where apprentices feel supported, respected and safe to learn. **Entry requirements** * Aged 18 years or over * Leaving Certificate with a minimum of five passes at Ordinary Level, including English and Maths **Skills and qualities we look for** * Curiosity and interest in how things work * Good communication and teamwork skills * Willingness to learn and ask questions * A positive, proactive approach * An interest in technology, engineering or problem solving **Practical details** * 39 hours per week, Monday to Friday * Some flexibility required in line with operational needs * A structured apprenticeship combining on\-the\-job learning with off\-site training **Opportunities within the wider Boliden Group** An apprenticeship with Boliden Tara Mines offers more than a qualification — it’s the start of a long‑term career. Graduates can grow onsite and across the **wider Boliden Group**, with skills valued nationally and internationally. **Inclusion matters to us** At Boliden Tara Mines, we are committed to a workplace where women and men feel welcome, supported and confident to succeed. We encourage applications from all backgrounds, including people who may not have previously considered a career in Electrical Instrumentation If you’re interested but unsure whether you meet all the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. If this career path interests you, we welcome your application. **Closing date: 19 June 2026** **If this opportunity interests you, we welcome your application via Boliden Careers.** \- Important! If you are a current employee within Boliden you shouldn't complete the application at this site, please log on to your Workday account

title: Content Marketing Specialist
description: Position Overview We are looking for a creative and enthusiastic Content Marketing Specialist to join our team on a part\-time basis. In this role, you will help create engaging content across social media, or blogs to grow our brand and build online communities. This is a flexible part\-time position ideal for someone who wants to gain hands\-on experience in content marketing.Key Responsibilities * Plan and create high\-quality content (posts, articles, or videos, graphics) for social media platforms and our website * Manage social media posting and community engagement * Help develop content calendars and support marketing campaigns * Work closely with the marketing team to support overall brand goals * Stay up\-to\-date with the latest content trends and social media best practices Requirements * Social media experience OR a major in computer\-related field (e.g., Computer Science, Digital Media, Information Technology, etc.) * Excellent writing and editing skills in English * Must have your own personal social media account(s) with a certain number of followers (at least 500\+ real followers across platforms) * Ability to create visual content using tools like Canva, CapCut, or similar, with proficiency in AI\-powered editing and clipping software (e.g., AI features in CapCut, Adobe Firefly, Runway, or equivalent tools) * Good analytical skills and basic familiarity with social media insights * No full\-time work experience required – recent graduates or students are welcome What We Offer * Competitive hourly pay * Flexible working hours (part\-time, approximately 15 hours per week) * Opportunity to grow your skills and portfolio * Creative and supportive work environment If you love creating content, have a strong personal social media presence, and want to help brands tell their stories, we’d love to hear from you! Please send your resume and links to your personal social media accounts when applying. Pay: From €18\.00 per hour Work Location: Remote

title: Corporate Actions Analyst
description: **Job Description** The Corporate Actions Analyst is responsible for the daily processing associated with Mandatory and Voluntary Corporate Actions. The successful candidate will assist with co\-ordinating the flow of required information between various stakeholders, including the Front Office, Portfolio Managers, Fund Accounting, Treasurer’s Offices and Custodian banks. The **Expertise** We’re Looking For * 1\-2 years of relevant Financial Services experience * Mandatory and Voluntary Corporate Actions processing experience preferable * Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent work experience The **Purpose** of Your Role The Global Corporate Actions team is responsible for all aspects of Corporate Actions processing. This includes Income, Mandatory and Voluntary events. The Corporate Actions Analyst is responsible for the daily tasks associated with processing Corporate Actions. This includes fulfilling the departments policies associated with corporate actions reporting and data management. The successful applicant will interact with many groups within the Fidelity organization and represent the business to customers and business partners. The **Skills** You Bring * Your knowledge of the key elements of Corporate Actions processing * Your knowledge of the Custody industry, Fund Accounting and Mutual Fund Operations * Your ability to manage multiple tasks effectively * Your ability to identify and escalate high risk items * Your strong written and verbal communications skills * Your time management skills * Your analytical and problem\-solving skills * Your Innovative mindset – you look for new ways to improve current processes and develop creative solutions. The **Value** You Deliver * Responsibility for high quality Soliciting of Voluntary corporate actions * Managing the communication of complex corporate actions to internal stakeholders * Interacting with many groups within the Fidelity organization and effectively representing the business to customers and business partners * Escalating issues appropriately * Demonstrating a strong team\-work ethic * Develop the knowledge required to grasp product and processing rules while working under the direction of more experienced colleagues. * Explore and communicate new ways of improving existing processes * Respond to research inquiries received from internal stakeholders and external vendors How Your Work **Impacts** the Organization The Global Middle Office acts as a servicing centre for FMR Co.'s trading desks globally, facilitating the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions with brokers and custodian banks, and providing daily cash estimates to Trading. It is also responsible for coordinating corporate action executions, ensuring the collection of income to the Funds, resolving position differences and facilitating securities lending activities. **Category** Investment Operations

title: Corporate Services Assistant
description: **Date:** 1 May 2026 **Location:** Dublin, IE, D01 W213 **Company:** Walkers Global **Overview of Walkers** ----------------------- Walkers is an international firm providing legal, corporate and fiduciary services to the global financial services community. Walkers Professional Services ("WPS") sets a “best in class” standard in the provision of corporate services to aircraft leasing and structured finance special purpose vehicles in Ireland. WPS is led by a team of over 50 highly skilled professionals drawn from accounting, audit, company secretarial, compliance and legal fields. The team combines a depth of specialised experience with a focus on consistently delivering high quality results to clients at all times. At WPS, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone can feel comfortable, happy and confident to be themselves. We believe that a diverse and inclusive business is more collaborative and innovative and provides great opportunity for our staff which in turn, allows us to better serve the needs of our clients. Additionally, we actively encourage participation in internal committees such as Corporate \& Social Responsibility and Diversity and Inclusion along with other firm wide initiatives. Walkers is an equal opportunities employer. **The Role** ------------ WPS is currently seeking a Corporate Services Assistant to join our dynamic and growing transaction team in Dublin. In this role, you will be part of a busy, client facing, professionally qualified team. This is an excellent opportunity to play a key role in a highly regarded and expanding organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous professional development and career progression within a collegiate team\-based environment. Please note, this is a fully onsite role and applicants must be willing and able to work five days per week in our Dublin office. **Duties and Responsibilities** ------------------------------- You will have a broad range of responsibilities including: * Assisting senior transaction executives and directors in the management of a portfolio of SPVs involved in structured finance and aircraft leasing; * Building and maintaining strong client relationships, ensuring a high quality, responsive and professional service to clients at all times; * Leading the process of on\-boarding new clients; * Assisting with a variety of company secretarial duties including coordinating board meetings, company incorporations, annual compliance, filings with the Companies Registration Office and other ad hoc duties; * Establishing and maintaining statutory books and records; * Assisting in the incorporation and setting up of new entities; * Attending board meetings and drafting non\-transactional board minutes; * Liaising with investment managers, arrangers, lessors, lawyers and other relevant parties; * Assisting with bank account openings, FATCA registrations, EMIR obligations and other necessary SPV transaction management tasks as required; * Contributing to internal projects and initiatives arising from changes in statutory or regulatory obligations; and * Ensuring compliance with internal policies and procedures, while actively contributing to process improvements and a positive office environment. **Knowledge and Experience** ---------------------------- * Prior office experience would be an advantage; * A university law or business graduate, with an excellent academic background; * A desire to work in a client facing environment where delivery of a professional service to both clients and other counterparties is of key importance; * Strong communication skills, an ability to think on their feet, excellent attention to detail and exceptional organisational skills are essential for this multi\-client role; * Conscientious, organised and a team player who can work on their own and as part of a wider team; and * Enthusiastic in nature, with a real interest in learning and developing their skills. **Benefits** ------------ We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Discretionary annual bonus scheme * 23 days annual leave (increasing to 25 days with service) * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme * Life Assurance * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees * Social Committee * Employee Assistance Programme * Wellness programme \#LI\-DJ1 \#LI\-Onsite

title: Cost Accountant
description: **Company Overview:** The O’Reilly Group comprises three companies: O’Reilly Precast, O’Reilly Barleystone, and O’Reilly Oakstown. O'Reilly Precast is a leading manufacturer of precast concrete products in the UK and Ireland. With a focus on innovative and efficient modular building solutions, we have established ourselves as market leaders in the construction industry. Our commitment to quality, reliability, and customer satisfaction sets us apart in the industry. **Job Description:** We are seeking a detail\-oriented and analytical Cost Accountant to join our team in the O’Reilly Group. This role involves supporting financial visibility by reviewing project costs, recording stock, assessing the cost impact of remakes and enhancing cost accounting processes. The ideal candidate will contribute strong analytical skills, a proactive mindset, and the ability to collaborate across departments. **Responsibilities:** * **Precast Project Review Analysis:** Maintain and review project revenue and costs versus budget. * **Remake review\-** Work with the Quality department to maintain list of re\-makes and quantify costs. * **Inventory Management:** Review stock takes and monitor stock count procedures. * **Group Product Costing:** Maintain costs for group products manufactured, and analyse changes in product design, raw materials, and manufacturing methods. * **Support Functions:** Provide support for production system (MPM) and project management system (PMP). * **Cost accounting systems**\- Drive the redesign and automation of cost accounting processes to improve efficiency. * **ESG**\- Assist in tracking and allocating environmental and social costs (e.g., carbon emissions, waste management) to support ESG performance metrics. **Requirements:** * Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel * Previous experience in a cost accounting, management accounting, or similar finance role. * Relevant third\-level qualification in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. * Part\-qualified or qualified accountant (e.g. ACA, ACCA, CIMA) is desirable. * Experience working in a manufacturing or construction environment is an advantage, but not essential. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks in a fast\-paced environment. * Good communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across departments. **Benefits:** * Competitive salary * Company pension * Bike\-to\-work scheme * On\-site parking * Private health insurance **How to Apply:** Please send your CV and cover letter to hr@oreilly.group. *O'Reilly Group is an equal opportunities employer. All qualified applicants are encouraged to apply.* Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Gym membership * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do you have experience working with inventory, stock control, or manufacturing costing? * Do you hold a degree or qualification in Accounting, Finance, or a related field? * Do you have a high level of experience using Microsoft Excel Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Customer Growth Associate, Early Careers, Google Customer Solutions (German Market)
description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * Experience in digital sales, client management, customer service, business development, customer outreach, marketing, consulting, digital advertising, or related field. * Ability to communicate in English and German fluently to engage with clients in the region. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Familiarity with Google Ads and online advertising, with Google Digital Workshop certification or equivalent experience. * Ability to build compelling narratives and utilize storytelling as a client engagement strategy. * Ability to influence stakeholders internally and externally to deliver impact. * Ability to own an advertising or media sales portfolio in Germany, and consistently overachieve quotas to drive growth. **About the job** ----------------- Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. As a Customer Growth Associate, you bring a passion for sales, knowledge of online media, and commitment to maximize customer success. You'll act like an owner, move with velocity through change, finding innovative and strategic ways to consistently deliver extraordinary and incremental outcomes for both Google and your customers. Building trusted relationships with customers along the way, you'll uncover their business needs and translate them into powerful solutions to achieve their most ambitious goals. You achieve as a team with sellers, shape the future of advertising in the AI\-era, and make a real impact on the millions of companies and billions of users that trust Google with their most important goals. Google Customer Solutions (GCS) sales teams are trusted advisors and competitive sellers who maintain a relentless focus on customer success by bringing the best Google has to offer to small\- and medium\-sized businesses (SMBs), which are the backbone of our communities. As a member of our team, you’ll have the opportunity to work with company owners and make a real difference in their businesses by helping them grow. Together, we help shape the future of innovation for customers, partners, and sellers...and we have fun doing it. **Responsibilities** -------------------- * Deliver against ambitious quarterly business growth and product growth goals. * Establish relationships via phone calls or email marketing and conduct regular phone consultations with clients to advise them on how to get the best return on their Google Ads investment. * Own a portfolio of small businesses by thoroughly understanding growth drivers identifying opportunities for growth, managing risks and building quarterly plans for achievement. * Drive customer growth by delivering outstanding customer sales experience and achieving customer business and marketing objectives. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Customer Service Associate - French & Dutch Speaker
description: The Customer Service Associate is responsible for providing exceptional service to inbound customer inquiries while also being responsible for sales of inbound accounts through inbound communication channels. They receive telephone calls/email/chat to handle all aspects of sales \& service, including price quotations, product and licensing information, solicitation, negotiation, and problem resolution. They collaborate with the sales team by providing administrative assistance and general support. The Customer Service Associate will use various computer systems to enter and track invoices as well as customer and sales data. This position is key in growing customer relationships, and is vital to our team. **Please note: C1 level and above in English, French \& Dutch (written and verbal) is required for this role.** **Who You Are:** * You are eager to get your foot in the door and embrace the fast\-paced buzz of the media world * Working for a global leader in digital content, supporting successful sales teams excites you and you thrive in a fast\-paced, service\-oriented environment where terrific customer support is critical * In this role, you would be responsible for providing service to inbound customer enquiries that are service\-related and delivering administrative assistance and general support to the sales team * Your goal is to help our customers, internal and external, work smarter * You’re an excellent communicator across multiple mediums like phones, chat, and email, and are focused on solving problems in a manner that enhances the overall customer relationship **Your Next Challenge:** * Receive and process inbound sales queries from clients via the phone, email and online chat * Sell to customers and engage them in discovery activities that determine their needs, suggests relevant products and services, and gather relevant customer information * Encourage and enable customers to self\-serve * Process, track, code and handle invoices and internal reports in a timely and accurate manner through the use of various computer systems * Provide creative solutions and strive to find more productive and efficient ways to process, track and handle invoice processing * Assist the sales team with other paperwork and administrative needs to enhance and streamline the workflow within the department * Answer phones promptly to ensure customers receive timely service and direct the customer to the appropriate sales person to assist them with sales queries * Handle customer requests for services related to password resets, download issues, technical questions, etc * Create research Customer Relationship Management (CRM) tickets **What You'll Need:** * Strong interpersonal, organisational and communication skills * Relevant customer service experience * Strong attention to detail * Strong customer service skills and the ability to build and maintain relationships via telephone and email * Proficient computer literacy (internet, Microsoft Office Suite including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, etc.) * Strong desire to learn * Fluency in written and spoken French, Dutch and English (C1 Level or above both written and verbal in both languages) * Ability to gather prospect and client information and to identify potential sales opportunities * Ability to effectively manage time \& complete multiple tasks in fast\-paced environment **Customer Service** The Customer Service team is responsible for providing an exceptional pre\- and post\- sale customer experience for inbound sales and service inquiries from customers. They receive telephone calls/emails/chats from customers to handle many aspects of sales and service, including price quotations, product and licensing information, negotiation, and problem resolution. They also collaborate with the sales team by providing administrative assistance and general support. **There’s a story in every picture, a narrative in every frame. We believe in the power of visuals. As a leading visual content creator, our three brands–Getty Images, iStock, and Unsplash–offer a full range of content solutions to meet the needs of any customer, whatever their size, location, or budget. Whether the goal is commercial or philanthropic, revenue\-generating or society\-changing, market\-disrupting or headline\-driving, our visuals help any brand, business, or organization communicate more effectively with their target audience and urge them to action. In other words, we know how powerful an image or video can be—and that it can move the world. **Working at Getty Images Our goal is to be one of the best places to work globally, which means we’re dedicated to providing experiences and resources that allow you to do your best work. Foundational to our culture are our Leadership Principles, which are the shared values that guide how we come together to do our work. We raise the bar and collectively bring solutions while exercising trust, transparency, and care. We actively reject biased behavior and are inclusive of different voices, perspectives, and experiences. We focus on our customers and deliver on our commitments and commercial goals. We foster a collaborative and supportive environment that prioritizes delivering results efficiently and using time wisely, so that all employees can achieve balance between commitments to the company and health and well\-being in our lives. We value the importance of rest and recovery ranging from company\-paid holidays to time away from work. Getty Images Holdings, Inc. is a publicly traded company, and its common stock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol "GETY". You may be eligible to participate in our employee Stock Purchase Plan, which allows employees to purchase company stock at a 15% discount to market. Our employees’ growth, development, belonging, and engagement are important investments. We offer learning experiences ranging from leadership development, diversity and inclusion training, mentoring programs, a high potential program, and professional and skills development. We prioritize staying connected in our hybrid\-working world and encourage participation in global morale events and local gatherings, as well as finding community in one or more of our Employee Resource Groups. Learning about our business is paramount, and our open and transparent culture means you’ll have direct access to experts and senior leaders via open forums, all hands, and content hours. Getty Images believes that diversity is critical to our success in moving the world and is committed to creating an inclusive, mutually respectful environment that celebrates diversity. We seek to hire based on merit, competence, performance, and business needs. Getty Images is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances.****

title: Cyber Security Analyst
description: My Dublin based client is recruiting for a Cyber Security Risk Analyst to join a growing team on a permanent basis. This role requires 1 day per week onsite. The Cyber Security Risk Analyst will be responsible for establishing and maintaining a strong security posture across the organisation. The team defines security strategies, frameworks, and controls, and ensures their effective implementation to protect information assets and manage security risks. **Responsibilities** * Act as a primary point of contact for information security matters related to service delivery and operational activities. * Promote awareness and understanding of information security principles, policies, and processes across the organisation. * Identify, assess, and monitor information security risks in collaboration with risk owners, ensuring appropriate controls are applied throughout the service and system lifecycle. * Evaluate the effectiveness of information security controls and ensure alignment with organisational policies, regulatory obligations, and recognised industry standards. * Develop, maintain, and improve information security policies, standards, and procedures. * Support third\-party risk management activities, including defining and assessing security requirements for suppliers and outsourced services. * Lead or contribute to security initiatives and improvement projects, working with cross\-functional teams to enhance security processes and increase automation where appropriate. **Skills / Experience Required** * A degree in computer science, information technology, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience. * 5\+ years of experience in information security or related IT roles, with strong exposure to risk management, security frameworks, and control design. * Broad knowledge of information security technologies, architectures, and operational processes, including information security management systems and industry standards (e.g. ISO 27001, COBIT, DORA, NIS2\). * Experience working with data, reporting, automation, or AI\-enabled tools to support security processes. * Experience managing or assessing security risks associated with third\-party suppliers, cloud services, and outsourced environments. * Proven ability to manage projects and influence stakeholders in complex organisational environments. * Strong communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, professional documentation and communicate effectively with technical and non\-technical audiences. **Benefits** Hybrid Working \- Only 1 Day per week required on site Healthcare Bonus Childcare Allowance Pension Sub Canteen

title: D365 Developer
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated 30 years in Technology Services and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver solutions that drive customer success. Our expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly changing Digital\-First world we live in. We foster strong partnerships with leading technology giants including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re An Award\-winning Employer Reflecting How Our Employees Are At The Very Heart Of Version 1 And What We Do * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core values driven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you! **Job Description** Design, develop, and implement Power Platform and D365 solutions based on established patterns and best practices, working with delivery teams and directly with customers. Contribute to driving cloud adoption and delivering innovative solutions, leveraging AI and low\-code technologies. Working as part of a team in mainly Agile delivered projects, you will be part of a collaboration, adaptability, and problem\-solving will be essential. **Key Responsibilities** * Build solutions using Power Apps (Canvas, Model\-Driven, Power Pages), Power Automate, and Dataverse. * Deliver customised and out\-of\-the\-box D365 CE solutions. * Work on diverse projects involving Power Pages, Copilot Studio, and integrations with other systems. * Collaborate with Agile delivery teams and contribute to CI/CD pipelines. * Provide technical advice and support for customer success. This role offers the chance to expand your expertise in Power Platform and D365 solutions. You'll work on diverse projects, from implementing out\-of\-the\-box D365 CE applications to creating tailored Power Platform solutions, including Power Pages and AI\-driven innovations using Copilot. Our varied customer base includes organisations at different stages of their low\-code journey, providing opportunities to deliver impactful and customised experiences. As a member of the MS Applications capability, you will have the opportunity to be part of a larger team and expand your knowledge and experience. **Qualifications** **Essential** * Proficiency with Microsoft Dataverse and model\-driven apps. * Strong communication and customer engagement skills. * Agile methodology experience. * Experience with Dynamics 365 business process, plugins and workflows (internal and Power Automate) **Desirable** * Copilot Studio experience or embedding copilot tools for users within solutions. * Experience of Power Platform Centre of Excellence (CoE) and governing/monitoring environments * Experience with Azure DevOps and CI/CD. * Experience developing and deploying Microsoft Teams Apps * Experience with Azure DevOps (or similar tools) for CI/CD and backlog/progress tracking * Broader Microsoft 365 tools (Teams, SharePoint, Power BI). * Experience integrating Power Apps solutions with APIs or third\-party solutions * Knowledge of Azure PaaS, APIs, and integration services. * Development experience (.NET, C\#, TypeScript). * Eligibility for UK Security Clearance. * Certifications (PL\-900, PL\-400, MB\-910\) **Additional Information** **Why Version 1?** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits. * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme. * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance. * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Pension, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme. * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies. * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat. * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more.

title: Data Analyst
description: **Company Overview** Holafly is a high\-growth scale\-up revolutionising how businesses and travellers connect to the internet abroad. Since 2018, we’ve empowered travellers in over 200 destinations worldwide with secure and reliable eSIM solutions. With a team of 500\+ professionals across multiple countries, we are scaling globally to support travellers with seamless, unlimited data connectivity. We’re not just connecting people—we’re enabling freedom and peace of mind, ensuring our users stay connected from the second they land, wherever their journey takes them. **About The Role** We are seeking a highly analytical and detail\-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for transforming complex data into understandable and conclusive insights. You will play a key role in training different business areas on the effective use of our Business Intelligence (BI) tools and advising them on how to maximize the value derived from our data. If you have a passion for data storytelling, strong communication skills, and enjoy empowering others with data, we encourage you to apply. **Responsibilities** * Collect, clean, analyse, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights. * Create clear, concise, and impactful visualisations, reports, and dashboards that effectively communicate findings to various stakeholders. * Develop and deliver training sessions to educate different business units on the functionality and best practices of BI tools. * Provide expert advice and guidance to the business area on how to leverage data to answer key business questions and drive informed decision\-making. * Collaborate with data engineers and other teams to ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and consistency. * Identify opportunities for process improvements and data optimisation. * Stay up\-to\-date with industry best practices and emerging trends in data analysis, BI tools, and data visualisation. **Qualifications** * Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role. * Strong proficiency in SQL. * Extensive experience with Business Intelligence (BI) tools (e.g., Looker Studio, Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Qlik Sense). * Excellent data visualization skills and the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and critical thinking abilities. * Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non\-technical audiences. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced environment. * Detail\-oriented with a strong commitment to data accuracy and integrity. * Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Business Analytics, or a Business related discipline. **Hybrid Modality** We focus on trust and performance. We are implementing a Two phased approach for our Hybrid Modality: Phase 1: While the teams are establishing ways of working, building trust, and developing their rhythm, we ask that you’re present in the office Three days a week. In\-person proximity is key to accelerating the forming stage. Phase 2: Once the teams are performing, we move to a more relaxed hybrid model. We ask for a minimum of one day per week in the office, centred around critical ceremonies such as sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives. **Benefits \& Perks** * 25 Days of PTO * 10% pension contribution * € 3000 for Medical/Health coverage * Self development budget of $ 500 During a trip to Thailand, Pedro and Lidia, the founders of Holafly, experienced the frustration of not having an internet connection. Inspired by their own experiences, they came up with a simple but brilliant idea: why not allow travellers to buy data plans for any destination from their home country? Our passion for providing easy and convenient connectivity to travellers around the world drives everything we do. We love our work and we do it with pride. Stay connected wherever you go.

title: Data Analyst
description: Are you a Data Analyst looking to make your next opportunity? I may have the job for you! **About Your New Employer** * Join a company that leverages one of the richest proprietary data sets in the world. * Work with a high\-calibre, passionate team dedicated to leading high\-impact projects. * Utilise advanced data tools and technologies to unlock customer value and drive business insights. **About Your New Job as a Program Manager** * Use unique data to power innovative tools addressing business challenges. * Write SQL/Presto/Trino/Spark queries to extract necessary data and use Tableau for data visualisation. * Take existing queries or narratives and optimise them for efficiency, building in automated checks and controls. * Handle support requests to ensure a bug\-free user experience and implement enhancements to internal data tools. **What Skills You Need as a Program Manager** * 3\+ years of experience with data analysis. * Previous experience working with SQL and data visualisation tools (e.g., Presto/Trino, Spark SQL, Tableau). * Comfortable working with PowerPoint and Excel. * Ability to turn data into insights and present to stakeholders. * Experience managing complex, multi\-workstream programs. * Expert knowledge of SQL, Hadoop (Presto/Spark), Git, and Tableau. **What’s on Offer as a Program Manager** * €58,000 \- €62,000 Annual Salary * 11\-month Temporary Contract * Hybrid Work \- One Day in the office per week **What’s Next** If you think this job is for you, apply now, or if you have any questions about the role or other IT Contract Jobs in Ireland, message me, Aaron Thornton, or click on apply. Looking forward to talking to some great candidates!

title: Data Analyst, Financial Enablement
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies \- from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups \- use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** The Internal System Org is at the core of Stripe to empower mission\-critical financial domains including Finance \& Strategy (F\&S), Accounting, Tax, Treasury, Product Experience, GTM. We are the Financial Enablement team, acting as the centralized data analytics team for Internal System Org. Enable and empower F\&S, GTM, Accounting, Product teams to achieve business goals, through dataset development, data analytics support, dashboards, AI, and operation efficiency enhancements. What you’ll do **Responsibilities** * Perform data analysis, identify trends, conduct research, build visualizations, all to deliver objective, critical insights that fulfill team stakeholders’ business needs * Work closely with cross\-functional teams, including Finance \& Strategy, Accounting, Product, Tax, User billing, Seller System, and Sales Ops teams, to align on financial goals and drive revenue growth * Be the data strategy partner for our business stakeholders * Reconcile complex dataset across complex finance systems * Coordinate cross\-functional projects to identify and drive operational efficiencies that support our stakeholders ability to run more efficiently and effectively * Work closely with Engineering and Product teams to help define product solutions * Leverage AI tools to improve team work efficiency * Conduct internal knowledge sharing sessions to strengthen team Who you are You’re passionate about solving business problems and making a direct impact on business. You thrive in environments with many moving parts and quickly pick up new domain knowledge and technologies (e.g. AI). We’re seeking someone with excellent communication and advanced SQL/analytics abilities. Strong background in Accounting/Finance or prior AI experience is highly preferred, though these can also be developed on the job. **Minimum Requirements** * Bachelor's degree in STEM major (i.e. Computer Science, Math, Stat, Engineering, etc.) and 4\+ years of prior working experience in data analysis fields * Strong analytical ability, proficiency with SQL (minimum of 3 years advanced SQL) and strong understanding of data relationships * Experience working with extremely large datasets * Outstanding communication skills * Expertise working cross\-functionally to drive business and operational goals * Excellent project management skills * Domain knowledge in Finance/Accounting/GTM **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience working in fast\-paced environments (e.g. high\-growth fintech/tech company, management consulting) * Experience working with PM, Engineers, GTM, Accounting Team or Finance Team * Experience working with financial datasets * Experience with visualization tools * Experience with AI tools (Claude Code, Kai, etc.) * Domain knowledge in FinTech Products * Qualification in Accounting/Finance or CPA/CFA certification In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €69,600 \- €104,400\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Data Center Operation Engineer (Graduate Program)-Dublin, Ireland
description: Alibaba Cloud is hiring students who are enthusiastic and eager to learn and grow to join the data center operation and maintenance team. As a data center O \& M personnel, you will be responsible for maintaining, upgrading, and managing the infrastructure, servers, assets, and security of the data center to ensure the stable operation of the data center. Through close cooperation with experienced technical experts and managers, you will continuously improve your technical and management capabilities. As a data center O \& M personnel, you will have the following responsibilities: * Develop standardized operation management procedures covering Facilities, IT, Assets, and Security for Alibaba Cloud’s large\-scale IDC data centers. * Explore and apply advanced O\&M technologies in digitalization, automation, and intelligence. * Oversee execution of operation standards for incidents, issues, changes, risks, maintenance, and personnel to achieve performance targets. * Drive internal and external communication and collaboration to optimize resource utilization, align with business goals, and meet customer SLA requirements. * Ensure the safety, stability, and efficiency of data center operations. * Contribute to R\&D, planning, solution design, construction, and delivery of IDC infrastructure (energy, buildings, power, HVAC, monitoring, networks, servers, etc.). * Stay current with technological trends and support the evolution of data center architecture to strengthen Alibaba Cloud IDC’s competitiveness. **Job Requirements** * Solid knowledge of computer software, hardware, and IT fundamentals; understanding of foundational IT network architecture; ability in data analysis and mining. * Strong learning ability, curiosity about emerging technologies, and adaptability to new environments. * Excellent communication, teamwork, and independent problem\-solving skills. * Fluent in Chinese and English (listening, speaking, reading, and writing) as a working language, or proficiency in other local languages. * Internship or project experience in cloud computing or the data center industry is preferred.

title: Deployment Project Coordinator
description: **Description** Responsibilities: * Act as the central coordination point for technical and business led projects, ensuring that project deliverables and the needs of all stakeholders are met. * Coordinate internal resources and third parties/vendors to deliver and execute flawless projects * Typical projects include new partners, new products, changes and enhancements to products or estates, functionality, features and services, large deployments, business processes, systems and business tools, and industry changes. * Manage full project life cycle from project scoping and objectives, business requirements gathering, through to business readiness, implementation and post\-implementation support and review * Own and manage all aspects of project governance including detailed project plans, management of risks and issues, including issue resolution and risk mitigation strategies), project reporting and escalation * Manage, control and report budget * Ensure their merchants, direct merchants and internal teams * Effectively manage changes through shifting business priorities, controlling project scope, resources and timing as needed. * Use appropriate
